title: BABIES FIRST! - 211info
text: JOSEPHINE COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH GRANTS PASS BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Babies First! is Oregon's public health nurse home visiting program that works to improve the health outcomes of vulnerable children through prevention and early identification of problems. The home visiting nurse can provide health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 715 NW Dimmick Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-5 with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include drug-exposed infant, prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, and/or concerned parent or provider. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (541) 474-5325 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 474-5325 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to register FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Josephine County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5:45pm, Friday 8am-noon Closed 8am-noon on the first Tuesday of each month TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on the corner of NW Dimmick and NW A Street Services: Case/Care Management * Families with Infants/Toddlers Home Based Parenting Education Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
